paid nearly 6% more than women for
similar work this year Iceland became
the first country in the world to pass
legislation not just to expose but to
tackle the gender pay gap companies with
over 25 employees like Reykjavik Energy
now have to prove they are paying men
and women equally for similar jobs
every job at the company must be
measured against a set of criteria this
produces a score for jobs with the same

raising the wages of its female
employees critics of the law point out
there will be significant financial
consequences for companies as they
